questions.question: Choose from the options a, b, c, and d given in Figures. the figure which shows the correct direction of the current.

options_data.explanation: In an electric circuit, the direction of the current is taken from the positive terminal to the negative terminal of the electric cell.

questions.question: Choose the incorrect statement.

options_data.option: A switch is the source of electric current in a circuit.
options_data.explanation: An electric cell is a source of electric current in the circuit.

options_data.option: A switch help to complete or break the circuit.
A switch helps us to use electricity as per our requirement.
When the switch is open there is an air gap between its terminals.

options_data.option: gases inside glass case of the bulb

options_data.explanation: The plastic clip is a bad conductor of electricity.

options_data.option: metal tip at the centre of the base.

questions.question: Paheli is running short of connecting wires. To complete an electric circuit, she may use a
In which of the following circuits A, B, and C given in Fig. the cell will be used up very rapidly?

solution_data.solution:
In figure (A) there is no bulb, the circuit is complete but it just connects the negative and positive terminal of the cell. And the cell is the source of electricity but in this case, we are not using electricity instead current is just flowing through the circuit not using up.
In figure (B), there is a bulb and the circuit is also complete hence the bulb will glow and will use the electricity and the cell will be used up.
But in figure (C) there are two bulbs hence consuming electricity at a much faster rate than the figure (B) and (A) resulting in a higher rate of consumption of cell and as a result, the cell will be used up more as compared to the case (A) and (B).

questions.question: Fig. shows a bulb with its different parts marked as 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5. Which of them labels the terminals of the bulb?

solution_data.solution:
Labels 3 and 4 marks the terminals of the bulb. 
Terminals are those parts through which the electricity is passed to the filament of the bulb. They are made up of metal because their work is to supply electricity to the filament. Terminals are like gates, if you want to go inside the room there is only one way that is passing through the gate. In the case of the bulb, if you want to enter the current into the filament you must pass it through the terminals. Without passing through terminals there is no other way. A bulb has also two terminals like a battery.

questions.question: You are provided with a bulb, a cell, a switch, and some connecting wires. Draw a diagram to show the connections between them to make the bulb glow.

solution_data.solution:
Steps to show the connections to make the bulb glow are as follows:

  1. There are two terminals of an electric cell. Connect the positive terminal of the electric cell to one terminal of the switch by the given connecting wires. The current starts from the positive terminal and passes through the switch.
  2. Now, connect the second terminal of the switch to the one terminal of the bulb by connecting wires. The current passes through the switch and bulb but still, the circuit is not complete, so the bulb will not glow.
  3. The last step is to connect the other terminal of the bulb to the negative terminal of the cell. This completes the electric circuit, as current starts from positive terminal goes to switch, if the switch is close it goes to bulb and then it completes the circuit by going to the negative terminal. This makes the complete electric circuit.

questions.question: Will the bulb glow in the circuit shown in Fig. Explain

solution_data.solution:
The bulb will not glow.
In this circuit, the switch is open that is circuit is incomplete, so the bulb will not glow. The switch helps to complete or break the circuit as it breaks the circuit when it is on "Open mode but it completes the circuit when it is on Closed mode, hence the switch helps us to use the electricity use as per our requirement. It happens because as there is an air gap between its terminal when it is open or on the "closed" mode.

questions.question: An electric bulb is connected to a cell through a switch as shown in Fig. When the switch is brought in the ‘ON’ position, the bulb does not glow. What could be the possible reason/s for it? Mention any two of them.

solution_data.solution:
The two possible reasons behind that are:

  1. The first possible reason is that maybe the filament inside the bulb is broken. The filament is the main component of the bulb which glows when the current passes through it. It glows because when electricity passes through the filament it got heat up. Heating causes the glowing of the bulb. But the extensive heating can break the filament and will result in not glowing off the bulb.
  2. The second possible reason is that the electric cell is completely used up or all the chemicals have been used already and there is no electricity left in it. As the cell is the only source of the power of the circuit, if it is completely used the circuit will not work and the bulb will not glow.

questions.question: A torch requires 3 cells. Show the arrangement of the cells, with a diagram, inside the torch so that the bulb glows.

solution_data.solution:
The current starts from the positive terminal of the cell and will come up back into the negative terminal of the cell. If a bulb requires three electric cells the most important thing is how you arrange the cell. The correct arrangement is that when the positive terminal of one cell is attached to the negative terminal of the other.
Now, it will work like a current from one cell will leave and will enter into another cell {from negative terminal of the second cell} there it will add with the current of the second cell and they both will leave {from positive terminal of the second cell} and then they will enter into third cell{ through the negative terminal of the third cell} after that all the current sum up and will go into the circuit from the positive terminal of the third cell.
The key point here is Current can leaves from the positive terminal of the cell only and enter into the cell from the negative terminal, we have to fulfill this two conditions to make this arrangement.

questions.question: When the chemicals in the electric cell are used up, the electric cell stops producing electricity. The electric cell is then replaced with a new one. In the case of rechargeable batteries (such as the type used in mobile phones, cameras, and inverters), they are used again and again. How?

solution_data.solution:
Rechargeable batteries are those batteries that are reusable after they are discharged by recharging them.
The main principle of a battery is that the chemicals inside the battery react and produce electricity, but once that chemical reacts completely there is no electricity left, then the battery is said to be a discharged battery.
But, there is a way to use discharged batteries again by reversing the reaction which produces electricity. That is, if we reverse the reaction which uses all the chemicals inside the battery and produces electricity, the chemical will again start generating electricity. This is the working of a rechargeable battery.
The reaction which consumes all the chemicals and produces electricity inside the cell is reversed and this is done by charging the battery. That is how rechargeable batteries work.

questions.question:
Paheli connected two bulbs to a cell as shown in Fig. 

She found that filament of bulb B is broken. Will bulb A glow in this circuit? Give reason.

solution_data.solution:
The bulb A will not glow.
As we know, if a circuit is not complete the bulb will not glow. The filament is the glowing part of the bulb as well as it is the connecting part. It completes the circuit within the bulb.
If the filament of bulb B is broken the circuit is not complete and hence bulb A will not glow. If any of the components is broken which completes the circuit the circuit will not work. The most important condition is to first complete the circuit. If any other bulb is also connected it will also not work because there is no complete way for a current.

questions.question: Why do bulbs have two terminals?

solution_data.solution: An electric cell has two terminals; one is called positive (+ ve) while the other is negative (– ve). An electric bulb has a filament that is connected to its terminals. An electric bulb glows when an electric current passes through it.

questions.question: Which of the following arrangement A, B, C and D given in Fig. should not be set up? Explain, why?

solution_data.solution:
A is the inappropriate set up as there is no resistance/bulb in the circuit.
In figure B the electric cell is connected to two bulbs and both bulbs will consume the cell at a very high rate but we are getting more light if we connect two bulbs.
In figure C the cell is connected to a bulb that will consume less electricity than the two bulbs and it will also give us light and energy will not be wasted.
In figure D the cell is connected to a bulb using a switch which is the most appropriate way to connect bulb, it will use the electric cell at a very low rate.
In all these cases we have seen that if the cell is getting used up we are getting output in the form of light. There is no wastage of energy in these three cases, but in figure A, a positive terminal of the cell is connected to the negative terminal of the cell. Hence, figure A is wrong as it wastes the whole energy.

questions.question: A fused bulb does not glow. Why?

solution_data.solution:
A fused bulb is a bulb whose filament is broken. The filament is the main glowing part if it is broken the bulb will obviously not glow because the circuit will be broken.
A bulb works on the basis that when current is supplied to the filament it heat-ups and this heating of the filament will make the filament glow like a glowing iron bar; that is for example when you heat an iron bar too much it turns into a red glowing iron bar.
So, the main working of the bulb is to supply the current to the filament will get heat up and start glowing. But when the current increases too much, the temperature of the filament also increases highly. This increase in the temperature of the filament can break or melt the filament which results in the breaking of the circuit. And this broken filament bulb is called the fused bulb. And that is why the fused bulb does not glow.

questions.question: Paheli wanted to glow a torch bulb using a cell. She could not get connecting wires, instead, she got two strips of aluminium foil. Will she succeed? Explain, how?

solution_data.solution: Yes, she will succeed in connecting aluminium foil because aluminium is a good conductor of electricity and can conduct the electric current through it. Hence the bulb will glow and the circuit will also be complete.

questions.question: Boojho has a cell and a single piece of connecting wire. Without cutting the wire in two, will he be able to make the bulb glow? Explain with the help of a circuit diagram.

solution_data.solution:
Yes, using the arrangement given below he can succeed in getting the bulb to glow.


In this arrangement, one terminal of the bulb should be connected directly to the positive (say) terminal of the cell and the other terminal of the bulb should be connected to the negative terminal of the cell using the given piece of connecting wire.

questions.question:
Fig. A and B, show a bulb connected to a cell in two different ways.
      

  1. What will be the direction of the current through the bulb in both cases? (Q to P or P to Q)
  2. Will the bulb glow in both cases?
  3. Does the brightness of the glowing bulb depend on the direction of current through it?

solution_data.solution:
1. We know that direction of the current flow is always from the positive terminal of the battery to the negative terminal through the whole circuit. So, in the first case, the direction of the current flow is from Q to P in the second case it is from P to Q.
  2. Yes, the bulb will glow in both cases as there is the flow of current through circuit in both the cases.
  3. No, the brightness of the glowing bulb does not depend on the direction of the current flow. It just depends on the amount of current and voltage through it.

questions.question: A torch is not functioning, though contact points in the torch are in working condition. What can be the possible reasons for this? Mention any three.

solution_data.solution:
If the torch is not functioning, though contact points in the torch are in working condition. Some of the possible reasons are as follows:

  1. The cells may have been used up.
  2. Cells might not have been arranged in the correct order.
  3. The bulb may have been fused.
  4. The switch might be faulty.
